programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog has problems when he goes to a small town on the Big Island of Hawaii to track down a fugitive. In a small town, everyone knows everyone else's business, making it impossible for Dog to hunt in secret.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the team search for Jonah, a violent and desperate criminal facing 20 years in jail. The trail goes stale when Dog runs into evasive family members and an angry girlfriend.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the team go after Kandice, AKA Candy, AKA Suga, a stripper who didn't make her court appearance. They convince one of her friends to lure her to a supermarket parking lot, where Dog has an easy time fetching the fetching young thing.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog leads the team on another adrenaline-filled chase in Denver. The fugitive, Robert, has a habit of address-hopping that makes him a difficult target, but Dog refuses to give up and finally tracks Robert down in his latest hideaway.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's time is running out in Colorado. After a month of helping his bail bondsmen friends around Denver, he's got to get his family back home to Hawaii. But Dog wants one more capture before they leave.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: They say you can't teach an old dog new tricks, but what if it's The Dog doing the teaching? Today's target is wanted on domestic abuse charges, but when Dog discovers she works for an escort service he opts for a new twist on an old ruse.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Other than bringing criminals to justice, one of Dog's loves is teaching the next generation of bounty hunters the tricks of the trade. Today he has a new student.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: There are few louder wake-up calls than coming-to in a jail cell with no idea how you got there. Add the media splashing the news of your arrest on every TV channel, and if your name is Lyssa Chapman you know you are in for a rough day.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today the cameras are turned around.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and company head to the Big Island in pursuit of two courthouse no-shows only to find that one of them appears to be posting status updates from Southern California.

programme.title: Someone They Knew With Tamron Hall
programme.desc: In 2001, University of South Carolina student Shelton Sanders disappeared while planning a friend's bachelor party; he was never seen again; when evidence leads to an arrest, however, prosecutors are left with a tall task.

programme.desc: The murder of a husband and his pregnant wife leaving a child birthing class leaves police thinking it was a random shooting until they find the real perpetrator weeks later.

programme.title: It Takes a Killer

programme.title: Earth Odyssey with Dylan Dreyer
programme.desc: Dylan Dreyer returns to explore the wildlife that lives in Malaysia.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Dr. Rae and Peter join field surveys of at-risk wildlife: Rae canoes Florida waters to count manatees; Peter joins a USFWS night survey near Denver to tag and release black-footed ferrets; together they dive a Bahamas sanctuary to document sharks.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: This episode is for the birds! Peter visits San Antonio Zoo to aid Guam kingfisher recovery. Dr. Rae observes piping plovers on Lake Michigan, then joins teams incubating condor eggs and raising chicks. Peter helps capture and release wild condors.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Dr. Rae and Peter explore aquatic wildlife and conservation: Rae meets North America's largest freshwater turtle in Georgia; Peter aids a Florida shark project; they join beluga research; Rae ends with a moving whale shark dive.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Dr. Rae and Peter reveal wildlife in action: fly drones to observe polar bears near the Arctic, use trail cams and thermal drones to find coatis in Arizona, spot an 8-foot shark by kayak off California, and use a dip cam to film a shark underside.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Join Dr. Rae and Peter as they explore American black bears. Dr. Rae visits New Hampshire for a vital den inspection with local wildlife experts, while Peter shadows a cub caretaker to see how abandoned cubs get a second chance.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Dr. Rae heads to Panama to explore the extraordinary, slow world of sloths. She tours a rescue and rehab center, meets the team saving these threatened animals, feeds orphaned babies, and helps rescue and release sloths back into the wild.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Peter and Dr. Rae visit the remote U.S.–Mexico border to uncover ocelots. With fewer than 200 left in South Texas, Dr. Rae joins ranch researchers and a renowned photographer, while Peter explores genetic efforts to grow the population.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Peter and Dr. Rae connect with harpy eagles, apex raptors of the Americas. Peter tours Florida raptor sanctuaries with conservationists; Dr. Rae visits Panama, where the harpy—national bird—holds deep cultural meaning.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Peter and Dr. Rae uncover Southeast reptiles in unlikely places: Dr. Rae aids an Atlanta sanctuary saving the Suwannee River alligator snapping turtle; Peter helps protect baby American crocodiles at a South Florida nuclear plant.

programme.title: TV-G
programme.desc: Dr. Rae and Peter spotlight coatis. Peter visits Brevard Zoo to see a breeding program boosting wild numbers. In Arizona’s Sonoran Desert, Dr. Rae uncovers Indigenous records of US coatis and tracks modern ones with scientists and photographers.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: Cops respond to a domestic violence call when a man pushes his girlfriend out of a moving vehicle because he did not want her to know it was stolen; a man is arrested for the use of drugs and several outstanding warrants.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: An ex-girlfriend breaks into her ex's home when he has a restraining order on her; cops search a car of a man who failed to use his light when changing lanes, but when cops search the car, they find marijuana and cocaine.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A young boy is found in a suspect's apartment after being kidnapped; a suspicious car is pulled over for having the wrong license plate, but when he talks to the officers, they notice he has crack-cocaine in his mouth.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A burglary suspect, who is in possession of several pieces of jewelry, is found and arrested; officers respond to a call involving a man who is approached by another man who is armed and has just robbed a bank.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: Cops pull over a stolen vehicle but the man bails out of the car and runs; another officer spots him and takes him to custody immediately.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: After officers respond to a shots fired call, the suspect flees his car on foot and the cops catch him with a loaded gun.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: Cops arrest three men in a car who have outstanding warrants; a man is pulled over for having a headlight out but runs as soon as he is stopped.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A distressed man at a local store says he is in fear for his life because his wife had become violent after returning home drunk; cops catch two people in the middle of a drug deal and one is arrested for possession of cocaine.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A dangerous car chase ends when a suspect rear ends a vehicle at an intersection, he is immediately apprehended; cops see men two men in a car late at night and as they approach the car they see a man put a bag in the center console.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: Cops recognize a woman who has a felony warrant for drug trafficking in a high crime neighborhood; a girl who was raped 14 times and has a child is arrested for prostitution.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A male suspect proves to be immune to the effects of a tazer; vandals are tracked down by both the police and the owner of the house toilet-papered; an armed man hearing voices calls 911 on himself.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A bicyclist takes off on foot and attempts to hide in his four year-old daughter's bed; police determine the identity of a human skull found in a park; a man passing through customs is held.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: Three car passengers are busted for three ounces of marijuana; police resolve a feud between owners of competing wedding chapels; two house burglars.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: An abandoned truck on the train tracks lead officers to a drunk driver; an abusive boyfriend is arrested for threatening his girlfriend with a brick; a bicyclist is discovered with a loaded gun in his pocket.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A drug dealer claims to be running from gunfire, not the police; a man is busted twice for trying to steal wishes from the Mirage Fountain in Las Vegas; an officer questions a couple.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A vehicle thief's run across state lines is brought to an end; officers stop a fight after a panhandler tries stealing; both parties of a domestic dispute accuse each other of illegal drug possession.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A suspended license prompts a motorcyclist to flee from police; police catch a male suspect with a spotty memory; two roommates are taken to jail for fighting over a live-in girlfriend; a driver hits a pedestrian.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Examining whether extraterrestrial beings helped save the nation during the Civil War; Abraham Lincoln experiences strange visions and premonitions; soldiers at Gettysburg see a ghostly image of George Washington.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Researchers ask if Cloud computing represents the rediscovery of an ancient repository of knowledge known as the Akashic Record; neuroscientist Dr. Dario Nardi measures the brainwaves of Dr. Deepak Chopra as he enters a state of deep meditation.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Many believe that the rise of communism led to secrecy surrounding ancient and modern extraterrestrial contact in Russia; theorists point to the archeological site of Arkaim as evidence that extraterrestrial visitations took place in Russia.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Ancient cultures from around the world believed that meteorites were sacred stones imbued with the power of the Gods; Ancient Astronaut theorists ask whether or not these space rocks could be pieces of extraterrestrial technology.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The native Hawaiians trace their ancestry to beings from the Pleiades star system called the Akua; a look at these islands and if they once had been inhabited by extraterrestrials.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: A memo from 1952 surfaces informing President-elect Dwight Eisenhower about the existence of a top-secret group of scientists, government officials and military personnel who are tasked with investigating reports of UFOs.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists suggest that compelling evidence of extraterrestrial visitation exists in China, the cradle of one of the earliest and most influential civilizations on Earth.

programme.desc: A sibling rivalry causes Ronald Salazar to become a killer; police chase a young serial killer.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When 37-year-old Lee Rotatori is found dead in her hotel room in 1982 with a single stab wound to the heart, police suspect the work of a professional; it takes four decades to identify her killer and, in the process, reveal a shocking truth.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When the body of 81-year-old Helen Gale is found in her burned-out car, investigators in Southfield, Mich., wonder who would murder this pillar of the community; it will be eight years before a blown alibi brings them face-to face with her killer.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When model Dawn Koons, 18, heads to California with dreams of stardom, her family back in Yonkers worry she's made a mistake; within months, her body is found in her bathtub; police have a suspect but unmasking her killer takes nearly 40 years.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When real estate appraiser Mike Williams mysteriously vanishes while duck hunting in the Florida swamps, authorities suspect he accidentally drowned. Did he?

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When 25-year-old school teacher Christy Mirack is found murdered in her apartment in 1992, there is no shortage of suspects.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a suspected serial killer preyed on women in small towns located on the banks of the Mississippi River until the murders abruptly ceased and went unsolved.

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When Sherry Leighty goes missing in 1999, her family is left wondering how a dedicated mother of three could vanish without a trace. A decade later, the cold case heats up.

programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy is charged with the murder of his sister, but his public defender believes the police coerced the boy's confession and that the real killer is still on the loose.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a teenage immigrant came to the United States to realize her dream of becoming a doctor. But a tragic twist of fate intervened, when she found herself an innocent victim of the drug trade. Kidnapped and held captive, she survived for days against all odds. Authorities arrested her captors, but they refused to reveal her whereabouts. The FBI and local police raced against time, determined to save the teenager's life. Their investigation revealed a complex story of drugs, betrayal and revenge.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In February of 1996, New Jersey millionaire Frank Black flew to Florida to close a major business deal before retiring. He never came home. Authorities in New Jersey sensed foul play and brought in the FBI. Agents learned of a mystery woman in Florida who was to meet Black. Phone records showed a flood of calls to Black from his business rival just before the disappearance.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1986, a small crime free Indiana town was turned inside out by the arrival of one dangerous stranger. After an officer was gunned down at point-blank range, the psychotic killer led investigators across the Midwest. No one knew where the gunman would strike next, even as his tally of murders and abductions grew. The FBI and state police raced against time to rescue America's heartland from this deranged gunman, before he killed again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On August 20, 1980, an unseen sniper murdered two young black men as they jogged with two white women in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police threw all their resources into finding a killer who left almost no clues behind. They met with FBI agents and detectives from around the country who had similar racially motivated sniper slayings. A month later, working off little more than a possible license plate number, police in Kentucky arrested Joseph Paul Franklin but the man escaped from custody.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 80's, Florida's cocaine imports were among the highest in the nation. Regional territories were enforced with intimidation and murder. Desperate to stop the violence, local authorities turned to the FBI for help. One dealer bowed to no one. He would stop at nothing to keep detectives and agents from building a case against him. When his organization began targeting the government itself, a city went on high alert.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Racial tensions in Buffalo in 1980 were at an all-time high. Six black males had been brutally murdered--two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets. Residents screamed for the police to stop this serial killer. With no suspects, local investigators turned to the FBI. Profilers were able to produce a psychological composite of the type of person the killer would most likely be. Their analysis paid off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A cunning predator was loose in rural South Carolinaabducting young women from their own front yards in broad daylight. He was also a sadist, calling one victim's family, taunting them, boosting and then dashing their hopes. Local investigators called on the FBI to help them find this clever serial killer. Like a game of chess, FBI profilers and forensic examiners were able to outwit the killer, and put an end to his deadly game of cat and mouse.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Local law enforcement became stumped when the body of an unidentified young woman turned up on a highway in their small Pennsylvania town. They turned to the FBI labs. The hi-tech lab enabled investigators and agents to identify the victim. Knowing who she was allowed agents to reconstruct her every move. Though the road to finding her killer was twisted and long, agents persisted.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Langley, Virginia, a gunman fired into rush hour traffic. The brazen daytime shooting of employees from the Central Intelligence Agency stunned the country. The victims seemed randomly selected, but the motive appeared to be political. The FBI was called in, but the nationwide search quickly became a far-reaching international manhunt. Agents processed more than 2000 leads and crossed an ocean in the effort to deliver justice.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: By April of 1995 in Washington, D.C., two officers had been shot and one had been murdered. Each had been sitting in their patrol cars when a man sneaked up and opened fire. The FBI was brought in to help catch the cop killer. Agents identified the man who was most likely the killer, but he was nowhere to be found. Months of surveillance operations around the country finally yielded a break; they found their suspect in Baltimore, MD.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two couples found themselves on the same deserted island paradise. But only one couple left alive. Buck Walker and Stephanie Stearns arrived in Hawaii on the Sea Wind, which belonged to the missing couple, Mac and Muff Graham. Buck and Stephanie were arrested and served time for boat theft. But years later, bones were discovered on the deserted island and identified as the remains of Muff Graham.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Labor Day weekend turned into a nightmare of murder as a cross-country crime spree terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and a teenage boy disappeared within hours of each other. Police believed the cases were linked, but didn't know how. They contacted the FBI for help. A trail of stolen vehicles and murder victims across seven states led agents on a hunt for two suspected killers. Their only hope of stopping them was to anticipate the killers' next move.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a California man was found dismembered in 1990, police suspected a local burglar, but they couldn't find him. Six months later, he was caught robbing an Arizona bank and sentenced to life. But prison couldn't hold him for long, and within a year he escaped. The FBI would lead a multi-agency manhunt for a killer on a desperate run through the tourist regions of the west.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished into the high desert, along with the two men hired to guard it. The FBI didn't know if the guards were participants or victims of an ambush. Answers lay somewhere in the thousands of square miles of Arizona's northwest territory. When the vehicle was recovered with three hundred thousand dollars and the drivers missing, suspicions that the crime was an inside job grew.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On the day before Thanksgiving, a mother discovered her 10 year-old son was missing from his school. No one knew how it could have happened. Within hours, the boy's entire family was frantically searching for him. The few leads in the case quickly turned cold and the FBI was called in to aid in the investigation. The search by agents and local authorities would cover a thousand miles to expose the kidnapper and find the missing child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, a triple murder terrified residents in the nation's capital. In a Georgetown coffee shop, three employees were shot to death. Investigators could find no immediate suspects and no clear motive for the attack. Evidence at the crime scene pointed in many different directions. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong, or an act of cold-blooded vengeance against one of the victims?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, a series of crimes in a small Iowa farming community left residents reeling in shock and fear. Two young men went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder, and then disappeared. By the time they were identified, they already had a twelve-hour lead. As the hunt for the two men expanded, the FBI committed its resources to tracking down the suspects. The pursuit would take agents across six states to catch the killers before they struck again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: He was a recluse who spread terror through the mail for 18 years. Two men died and more than a dozen were injured, but Ted Kaczynski wasn't known as the Unabomber until his brother tipped off the FBI. Kaczynski's “Unabomber Manifesto,” published in major newspapers, set off a flurry of activity for the FBI: writing analysis and comparisons, DNA gleaned from a single postage stamp and finally, Kaczynski's arrest at his tiny cabin.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In November of 1994, when a Wells Fargo armored van disappeared from a Glendale, Arizona mall, local authorities and the FBI scrambled to recover it, and its cargo of nearly a million dollars. Six hours later, agents found the van, its driver shot dead inside and the money gone. It was a perfect hit by professionals who left no clue behind. Months passed before a solid lead developed. In January, a tip led agents to look into a former prison guard.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Beginning in California in 1995, a serial killer targeted vulnerable women. At first charming and generous, he quickly became violent. He was brutal with his kills and quick in his getaways. Traveling across the country, he struck in Mississippi, Louisiana and Florida. The FBI and local police needed to stop this cross-country killer before more women fell victim to his insatiable rage.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Claude Dallas lived the life of a cowboy. A loner and distrustful of authority, he lived off the land, poaching wildlife and selling the hides. But when two game wardens confronted Dallas about his deeds, they were shot and killed. After a massive manhunt, Dallas was captured and convicted, only to escape months later. Making the FBI's Ten Most Wanted list, Dallas eluded the law for almost a year until the FBI tracked him down in a small town in California.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1990's drug gangs terrorized Harlem, New York neighborhoods. When a 10-year-old boy fell victim in the crossfire, NYPD detectives and FBI agents clamped down on the problem. They formed a task force called C-11, designed to dismantle the gangs. In an environment of violence and fear it would be difficult to identify, arrest and convict those involved in the secretive and deadly world of big money drugs.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a Delaware woman vanished in 1996, family and friends discovered her secret life. The appointments secretary to the Governor had carried on an illicit affair with a wealthy married man. Agents suspected that her powerful ex-lover was responsible, but they had little physical evidence to support their theory. Investigators never found the woman's body or the murder weapon. The FBI's only hope was to go undercover, and turn brother against brother.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a Delaware woman vanished in 1996, family and friends discovered her secret life. The appointments secretary to the Governor had carried on an illicit affair with a wealthy married man. Agents suspected that her powerful ex-lover was responsible, but they had little physical evidence to support their theory. Investigators never found the woman's body or the murder weapon. The FBI's only hope was to go undercover, and turn brother against brother.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, a series of crimes in a small Iowa farming community left residents reeling in shock and fear. Two young men went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder, and then disappeared. By the time they were identified, they already had a twelve-hour lead. As the hunt for the two men expanded, the FBI committed its resources to tracking down the suspects. The pursuit would take agents across six states to catch the killers before they struck again.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 90s, Washington, D.C. police were losing the battle against street gangs who enforced their territories with lethal violence. The homicide rate in the nation's capital climbed to the highest in the country. Local police turned to the FBI for help, but agents soon found themselves in the direct line of fire. In the fall of 1994, a gang member walked into police headquarters, killed two FBI agents and one detective, then turned the gun on himself.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young mother and her infant son mysteriously disappeared from their home. The woman's husband was the only suspect, but he was eventually cleared. There was no solid evidence that a crime had even been committed. Six months later, a local farmer found the remains of the missing woman and her child. Both had been murdered. Agents and local police had only one tiny piece of evidence, an unidentified hair found in the murdered woman's car. The hair was too small for analysis.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A 1981 armored car robbery near New York City left one guard dead and another critically wounded. Police stopped suspects nearby and another shoot-out ensued, claiming the lives of two officers. Local police and the FBI suspected a large group of domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. They would try to ferret out these heavily armed criminals who moved in a shadowy underworld and were not afraid to kill authorities.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A stranger snatched an eight-year-old girl as she played in her Tucson, Arizona neighborhood, leaving behind an abandoned bicycle and a frantic mother. Hundreds of citizens and police officers fanned out across the region, hoping to find some trace of the young girl or her abductor. Within hours, a suspect emerged; a drifter who was out on parole for abducting and molesting children. He was nowhere to be found and time had run out for the innocent child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished into the high desert, along with the two men hired to guard it. The FBI didn't know if the guards were participants or victims of an ambush. Answers lay somewhere in the thousands of square miles of Arizona's northwest territory. When the vehicle was recovered with three hundred thousand dollars and the drivers missing, suspicions that the crime was an inside job grew.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a summer afternoon in upscale Lodi, California, a 12-year-old girl is abducted from her home. Scores of police officers and FBI agents converge, determined to find the young girl before it is too late. When the kidnapper's car is discovered in a field near Lodi, police dogs, helicopters, and hundreds of volunteers are employed in a round-the-clock search. It would take a community united in concern for one of their youngest to find the missing child.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On August 20, 1980, an unseen sniper murdered two young black men as they jogged with two white women in Salt Lake City, Utah. Police threw all their resources into finding a killer who left almost no clues behind. They met with FBI agents and detectives from around the country who had similar racially motivated sniper slayings. A month later, working off little more than a possible license plate number, police in Kentucky arrested Joseph Paul Franklin but the man escaped from custody.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Residents of the small town of Noel, Missouri were shocked when one of their banks was robbed. When the bank president was nowhere to be found, the residents were outraged. The FBI discovered that things are not always as they appear. Minute clues led the agents to the body of the bank president. With no suspects, the FBI relied on dogged determination and modern forensics. Agents slowly pieced together tiny shreds of evidence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Local law enforcement became stumped when the body of an unidentified young woman turned up on a highway in their small Pennsylvania town. They turned to the FBI labs. The hi-tech lab enabled investigators and agents to identify the victim. Knowing who she was allowed agents to reconstruct her every move. Though the road to finding her killer was twisted and long, agents persisted.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Racial tensions in Buffalo in 1980 were at an all-time high. Six black males had been brutally murdered--two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets. Residents screamed for the police to stop this serial killer. With no suspects, local investigators turned to the FBI. Profilers were able to produce a psychological composite of the type of person the killer would most likely be. Their analysis paid off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: California Highway Patrolman George Gwaltney discovered the body of 23-year-old Robin Bishop near her car on a remote desert highway. Although he proclaimed his innocence the FBI lab experts were able to link tool marks found on the frame with tools found in Gwaltney's home. Through the match of hairs and fibers and a unique test for detecting and identifying sperm, Gwaltney was placed at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, a triple murder terrified residents in the nation's capital. In a Georgetown coffee shop, three employees were shot to death. Investigators could find no immediate suspects and no clear motive for the attack. Evidence at the crime scene pointed in many different directions. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong, or an act of cold-blooded vengeance against one of the victims?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Memphis, Tennessee, a horrible crime terrified local residents. When a wealthy young mother was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The twenty-five year old victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband, and several spurned boyfriends. All were on the FBI's short list. To find the woman and her abductor, investigators would have to first determine the motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a series of brutal bank robberies and murders in south Miami during the mid-1980's, the FBI embarked on an exhaustive eight-month investigation. With little evidence and no suspects, agents were willing to try anything to find the violent perpetrators. On April 11, 1986, agents conducting routine surveillance spotted the vehicle used in the bank robberies. They attempted to arrest the two suspects in the car. What followed was the bloodiest firefight in FBI history.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On June 21, 1964, three civil rights workers, two white and one black disappeared in Mississippi while passing through Neshoba County. A massive FBI investigation led to the recovery of the bodies of the slain men and physical evidence was recovered and analyzed. The FBI labs confirmed that all but one of the bullets used to kill the men came from the same gun. The FBI gained the trust of an informant, whose information led to the conviction of seven local Ku Klux Klan members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Anchorage, Alaska, a young mother and her two daughters were sexually assaulted and brutally murdered. There was only one piece of forensic evidence: a washrag carrying pubic lice that the killer used to clean himself after the crime. Employing an unusual and original method, an FBI expert combed through all the hairs and fibers in the apartment. He was not only able to determine how long each hair had been there, but which hairs came from the killer and how the crime proceeded.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On Mississippi's Gulf Coast, a judge and his politician wife were brutally murdered in their home. The killer left few clues and the investigation led nowhere. But the FBI was patiently waiting in the wings. Once it was determined that federal laws had been violated, they pounced on the investigation. They uncovered a corrupt local government and a group of violent criminal conspirators known as the Dixie Mafia, responsible for fraud, money laundering and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two couples found themselves on the same deserted island paradise. But only one couple left alive. Buck Walker and Stephanie Stearns arrived in Hawaii on the Sea Wind, which belonged to the missing couple, Mac and Muff Graham. Buck and Stephanie were arrested and served time for boat theft. But years later, bones were discovered on the deserted island and identified as the remains of Muff Graham.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A serial killer's rampage was discovered when the bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves that dotted the Alaskan wilderness. The killer taunted his victims, hunting them like animals before shooting them down. He was at home in the rugged terrain. But the hunt for him began 3000 miles away, where FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind.

programme.desc: Wedded couples vow to stay together, until death do they part. This series looks at what happens when a bride or groom causes their beloved’s demise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In little more than a month, two women who lived in the same apartment complex were brutally murdered. The similarities of the crime scenes led police to conclude they were dealing with a serial killer who harbored an intense hatred of women.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, nursing student Tamika Huston went missing from her Spartanburg, South Carolina home. A tip led detectives to her car and they find an unknown house key that could help solve the case.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, Judy Southern arrived home from work and was shot by a gunman waiting within. Her husband Allen arrived shortly afterwards, called 911 to report his wife had been shot and drove her to the hospital.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2007, the Florida mobile home of Effie and Michael Ratley catches fire and Michael heroically rescues his wife and infant son. A month later, his wife is found beaten to death in a bedroom of his parents' home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A lifelong resident of the tiny town of Lefroy, Tasmania was murdered outside his own home. Robbery appeared to be the motive, but with no suspects, the investigation came to a halt.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A bullet-riddled car, a missing driver, and no witnesses. Was this an ambush or a random attack? Had the victim been abducted or was she dead? The answers lay in a unique clue, so tiny it was measured in millionths of a meter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, college co-ed Jenna Verhaalen was found dead in her Bryan, Texas apartment and petechial hemorrhages in her eyes indicated that she was strangled.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1990s, the media dubbed Richard Rogers the 'Last Call Killer' because he targeted men in gay bars who were obviously intoxicated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 13-year-old girl went missing from her Colorado home, and the only evidence¾the kidnapper¾left behind was three fingerprints on a window screen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was brutally murdered in her home, and the only witnesses to the crime were the family dogs. An expert in canine behavior was convinced the killer knew both the victim and the animals, and he was determined find out what the dogs had seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the early hours of a morning just before Christmas, a college co-ed was abducted from the parking lot of her apartment. Her body was found later that day; she'd been sexually assaulted and then shot at point blank range.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial killer was on the loose and police had to find him before he struck again. Their most promising lead was an unusual one: a bloody fingerprint on the body of one of the victims.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman went missing, friends and family were determined to find her. Their worst fears were confirmed weeks later when her body was discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, when teenager Tara Munsey goes missing after a work shift at a Virginia restaurant, it is unknown if she ran away or if she was the victim of foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 54-year-old Gayle Isleib was ambushed in her Manchester, Connecticut driveway and shot to death. Now police must determine if love had turned into obsession... and a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2006, Darlene VanderGiesen received threatening emails and then disappeared. Tracking the source of the emails led police to the home of Daphne Wright, where they believe a murder was committed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had ingested a massive amount of cyanide. An unlikely clue _ a flaw on a mailing envelope _ exposed a murderer who was willing to kill innocent people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, Rebecca Barney and her soon-to-be ex-husband Fred were found shot to death in their Tulsa, Oklahoma, home, which had been set on fire.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy socialite died after falling down the stairs, witnesses said one thing and evidence indicated another. To find out what really happened, investigators turned to forensic science, a physicist and an expert in accident reconstruction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2007, Brian & Beverly Mauck were found dead in their Graham, Washington home. Using a bloody palm print and shoe prints at the scene, investigators focus their attention on Daniel Tavares, who had previously been inking a tattoo on Brian's back.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An assistant manager of a Florida steakhouse is stabbed to death. It appears to be a robbery gone wrong, but a bloody fingerprint reveals that he knew his killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, young mother Tammy Tatum was found sexually assaulted and murdered in her Longmont, Colorado apartment. Police initially suspected her estranged husband, but did not have enough evidence to charge him and the case went cold.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, Colorado real estate developer Alan Helmick was found shot to death at home by his wife Miriam, who then became the prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Security cameras in a casino tracked a young woman's movements until shortly before she disappeared. She was never seen again, but through the evidence she left behind, she was able to tell investigators what happened to her and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, funeral director Lonnie Turner, Sr. was found shot to death. His son Lonnie Jr. became the prime suspect, particularly after it was discovered that the murder was committed with his gun. However, he had an alibi for the time of the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a respected police officer was murdered in her own home. The crime went unsolved for more than a decade, and then a newly formed Cold Case Unit took a fresh look at the evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim was a self-made man who never minced words. Perhaps he was murdered by a disgruntled client or employee. Perhaps the racy photos in his safe held the clue to solving the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A security guard disappeared from his post without a trace; his remains were found a year later in a remote camp site.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, Paul Gruber's daughter suspected something was wrong when she received birthday cards addressed from Gruber, on which the handwriting wasn't Gruber's.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Tracey Frame, 35, was convicted of killing David Nixon in April 2002 after he planned to end their relationship.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Doctors don't know why the young scientist is gravely ill. When tests finally reveal the cause, it's too late to save him. Police hope that lab analysis of his hair, showing when attempts were made on his life and what was used will lead to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2001, Ginger Hayes and her infant son Nicholas were abducted during a carjacking and the crime had been reported by a witness within minutes of occurrence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman had been sexually assaulted, stabbed repeatedly, and left for dead. When someone who fit that description practically turned himself in, police were sure they had their man... until the DNA evidence proved them wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When¾two women went missing and were later found brutally murdered, police wondered if they were victims of a hate crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A six-year-old girl ran and hid when she saw her grandmother being beaten to death, but the man followed, beat and assaulted her. She said the assailant was her uncle, who was convicted. She recanted her testimony years later.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: LEADERS with Francine Lacqua:Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
programme.desc: What drives the world’s most influential minds? In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, Bloomberg’s award-winning anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas. Discover what inspires them, how they navigate power and the lessons they offer on defining success. Insightful, cinematic and elegantly crafted, Leaders goes beyond the headlines to reveal the human side of leadership.

programme.desc: Un chico pierde todas las cosas que lo motivan a vivir. Se ha quedado sin trabajo, casa y familia, pero en su recorrido por las calles se encuentra a Cometa, un perro callejero.

programme.title: Desierto adentro
programme.desc: Un hombre ha cometido un gran pecado contra Dios y está convencido de que sobre sus hijos caerá la condena de una muerte prematura.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: Maria do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Gregorio se siente traicionado por su madre. Efraín defiende a Santiago de Silvia y sus acusaciones.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: Historia bíblica que sigue a Josué mientras lidera a los hebreos hacia Canaán. Josué enfrenta diversos desafíos, incluyendo la oposición de Samara, quien está enamorada de él.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: Pilato se prepara para su encuentro con el César. Jesús se despide de sus apóstoles antes de volver al cielo con Dios.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: Los apóstoles regresan a Jerusalén para llevar la palabra de Jesús al pueblo. Anás y Caifás arrestan a Pedro por haber hecho milagros sin su consentimiento. Esteban es apedreado por Caifás para evitar que siga los pasos de Jesús.

programme.title: Jesús
programme.desc: La trayectoria de Jesucristo antes de la resurrección. Sus milagros y el calvario de la cruz. Las vidas de quienes fueron curados por Jesucristo y quiénes eran los apóstoles.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Gioconda queda fascinada con Álvaro. Malvina se rehúsa a que Isaura trabaje en harapos y que sea humillada por Leoncio. Paulo exige que traigan de vuelta a Helena. El ímpetu de Leoncio reluce cuando Isaura manifiesta su amor a Álvaro.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Miguel está encarcelado con la Fiera de Macabú, quien está abrumado porque está a punto de ser ejecutado. Tomasia visita a Miguel para ofrecer su apoyo. Leoncio amenaza con desfigurar a Isaura. Paulo besa a Rosa.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Malvina halla como insostenible la situación que vive con Leoncio e Isaura. La Fiera de Macabú le cuenta a Miguel su triste historia. Bernardo y Henrique hacen hallazgos llenos de fortuna. Belchior alegra a Isaura con un mensaje.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Rosa le recrimina a Sebastián sus pocos cuidados y atenciones. Coqueiro confiesa a Miguel sus líos de faldas. Paulo exige a Sebastián que le pague la dote de Helena. Leoncio le da un ultimátum a Isaura para entregarse a él.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Chico nota la pasión desmedida de Leoncio por Isaura. El patrón se niega a desprenderse de ella y no quiere hacer la subasta. Henrique y Bernardo tratan de vender sus diamantes.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Los perros son un grupo de salvajes que quieren muertos a los turcos, y ahora son la principal amenaza. El juicio de Ulu Ece y Kaya por traición tiene que posponerse, sin embrago, Alpagu le da un castigo ejemplar y Akkiz se queda en el palacio.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Kaya hace un pacto con los perros y les pide que mate a su padre y a sus hermanos para convertirse en Khan. Uno de los perros visita el palacio y les hace pensar que los guerreros están muertos. Tanto Akkiz como Batuga deben escapar de sus prisiones.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Akkiz logra escapar del palacio y va en busca de Batuga. Alpagu y sus hijos logran escapar de los perros, pero gracias a la traición de Kaya, vuelven a encontrarlos. Kaya se ha nombrado el nuevo Khan del cielo y planea hacer las cosas muy distintas.

programme.title: Destan
programme.desc: Las duras y frías estepas de Asia Central son testigos de la épica historia de amor entre Akkiz, una guerrera legendaria nacida en la montaña y el príncipe Batuga, a quienes un pasado muy cruel, los une para cambiar el destino del pueblo turco.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan is exploring the tiny village that for 45 years has been linked to Olympic glory. During a chilly visit to the Adirondack mountains, Nicole tours the Olympic venues that continue to make Lake Placid proud.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In 2023, floods devastated the mountain town of Ludlow, Vermont. Today this community and the surrounding area are rebounding and Shayna Seymour pays a visit as it basks in the glory of autumn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan pays a visit to renowned chef Jacques Pepin at his Madison home, where they talk about food, art, and his extraordinary life. She goes birding in Hammonasset State Park, and savors the fresh fried seafood at the Clam Castle.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: An angler’s paradise in the summer; a skier’s haven in the winter. Erika Tarantal visits the southern Vermont town of Manchester, Vt., and brings us a taste of its historic charm, outdoor adventure, and embrace of the arts.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Mount Washington towers above it, and the rocky landscape provides a natural playground. Ted Reinstein is exploring the village of North Conway, N.H. – and the region around it – in all its autumn splendor.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Saddleback, “sled-heads,” and spectacular winter views of Mooselookmeguntic Lake. Tonight, Anthony brings us the story of the town of Rangeley fighting back after the closure of Saddleback Mountain, now reopened and thriving.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Explore the Main Streets and backroads of Rochester New Hampshire.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You know it as the home of the Headless Horsemen, but the town of Sleepy Hollow offers much more than spooky lore. Ted Reinstein is venturing just over the New England border to this haven by the Hudson River.

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: Join host Hannah Cumler as she celebrates Leelanau County. Together, you’ll dive into Lake Michigan for freshwater surfing, sip the region’s best wines, hike the iconic Sleeping Bear Dunes, and travel to a remote island to see history in the making.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight an extraordinary story of a community coming together to save the local general store, Coburns’. Ted also hits a local diner; checks out a small community ski hill; and takes in the glory of one of the most photographed buildings in Vermont.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is exploring the Vermont side of the Upper Connecticut River Valley, where he finds the traditional, the quirky, and even the (literally) cartoonish.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Ted wants to live to 100 with his beloved wife beside him. But his oral health threatens his future. Kristy’s a FIXT staffer desperate to smile big on her wedding day. Will love help them face their fears?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You know the Whites and the Greens, but there’s another major mountain range in the Northeast – the Catskills. Tonight Ted Reinstein ventures westward to explore the rugged beauty of the region.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Exploring the Islands of Maine on a Sailing Ship.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tiny Fryeburg, Maine, balloons in size each fall when more than 200,000 visitors pass through the gates of the legendary Fryeburg Fair. Tonight Erika Tarantal tries her hand at the unique sport of “skillet-tossing” and helps prep a show cow for a moment in the spotlight. From the midway, to the 4-H and baking competitions, to the mouthwatering fair food, Erika brings us the sights, sounds, tastes, and tradition of Maine’s oldest fair.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett is heading north to board a ferry – several, actually! – to explore a few of the hundreds of islands in Maine’s Casco Bay. First up: waterfront tents on tiny House Island, and a barracks-turned-inn on Great Diamond Island.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Ted wants to live to 100 with his beloved wife beside him. But his oral health threatens his future. Kristy’s a FIXT staffer desperate to smile big on her wedding day. Will love help them face their fears?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Maine’s North Woods is the largest tract of undeveloped forest land east of the Rocky Mountains. The vast expanse of trees has the ability to offset 500 million metric tons of carbon dioxide from our atmosphere, which is equal to taking a million cars off the road-- for a century! Tonight, Ted Reinstein reports on the New England Forestry Foundation’s efforts to spread the word about the North Woods’ untapped potential to address the climate crisis.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Mount Desert Island is a magnet for hikers, campers and sightseers. The Schoodic Peninsula – not so much. Surprising, because the two places have so much in common, including stunning coastal views and untamed forests. The difference: the Schoodic has no crowds. Tonight, we explore the peninsula, sampling local specialties like pickled wrinkles and dulse, revisit one of our favorite destinations, Wild Blueberry Land, and groove to Flash in the Pans – the hottest steel drum band in the state.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A glimpse of unspoiled Maine for those “from away.” Tonight, Anthony Everett takes us up to Maine’s scenic St. George Peninsula. In Port Clyde, we get the history of a lighthouse saved by local volunteers and a labor of love. In Owls Head, Anthony takes to the skies in a vintage bi-plane and catches a ride in a 1929 Rolls Royce. We sample a taste of Africa in the middle of Rockland at Me Lon Togo, where the chef is also the musical entertainment. Finally, we spend time with a woman who is 102-years-young and still hauling lobsters.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s a milestone year for WCVB (50 years) and Chronicle (40 years)! Tonight we meet others who are marking major milestones this year. The Boston Gay Men’s Chorus is celebrating 40 years on the scene; we talk to original members who recall the early days. Marking 100 years are family-owned businesses George’s Shoes in Jamaica Plain and Lopez the Florist in Dorchester (where Erika Tarantal gets a lesson in flower-arranging). Tune in for those milestone stories and more. Then stay tuned for a prime-time special, WCVB: 50 Years.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes and inside-the-workshop journey of what goes into making music. We discover the precise and time-consuming task of making violins in Boston’s North End. From bass-making in Billerica to ancient Greek instruments in Lowell, we meet artisans and enthusiasts collecting, crafting, and restoring special instruments. We also get a glimpse at how music heals with therapy in Lawrence, and visit a church group in Brookline helping to launch a new generation of Black musicians in Roxbury.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Nicole Estaphan “guides” us through stories all about maps (including that Californian now selling them in Pawtucket, R.I.)

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Local News
programme.desc: Get the latest news and weather from WCVB.

programme.title: LIVE: On The Record
programme.desc: Congressman Richard Neal is the guest on WCVB’s On the Record this week. Sharman Sacchetti and Ed Harding are the hosts. Political Analyst Adrian Walker and Republican Political Analyst Ginny Buckingham join the roundtable discussion.

programme.title: LIVE: CityLine
programme.desc: A Conversation with Ayanna PressleyThis week on CityLine, a conversation with Congresswoman Ayanna Pressley. The representative for Massachusetts' 7th Congressional District sits down with host Jessica Brown to discuss the big on issues Capitol Hill, from immigration to the war in Iran.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We may not be able to run 26.2 miles, but we can definitely travel that distance in search of good food. Tonight, the Chronicle team is eating our way from Hopkinton to Boston.

programme.title: Third Coast
programme.desc: There's nothing like Benzie County! Join host Hannah Cumler as she celebrates the powerful women behind a thriving family resort, the Michiganders running the Midwest’s first grain-to-glass distillery, and toasts this can't-miss destination.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A spotlight on Marathon Runners and their stories.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Scanned the real estate listings lately? Inventory is low, prices are sky-high, and some buyers are going to extreme lengths to lock in a purchase. Tonight Shayna Seymour surveys the market. We meet a Worcester mom who was forced to widen her search to find an affordable home, and families choosing to remodel rather than compete with other buyers. A local home inspector shares a few hints (and horror stories). Finally, Shayna gets a peek inside a $7 million mansion that offers a taste of the celebrity lifestyle.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Water changes from a gray to a blue-green, flowers begin to bloom, and with restaurants reopening it can only mean one thing: it is spring on Martha’s Vineyard. Shayna Seymour takes a stroll near the Edgartown Harbor light, checks in at the Harbor View hotel, visits the Oak Bluffs Gingerbread Houses, and warms up with a bowl of conch chowder. She also tours the Martha’s Vineyard Museum where she learns about the little-known history of Black and Native American whaling captains.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Ted wants to live to 100 with his beloved wife beside him. But his oral health threatens his future. Kristy’s a FIXT staffer desperate to smile big on her wedding day. Will love help them face their fears?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan meets a local mom who has defied the odds and is alive 10 years after a diagnosis of Stage 4 lung cancer, thanks in part to targeted gene therapy.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan meets a student/musician from the Berklee College of Music who was diagnosed with a tumor. Nicole also meets a young woman, one of the 1.5 million Americans with lupus, who underwent a groundbreaking new therapy that a local doctor says is a gamechanger. Tonight Nicole brings us these and other stories of patients and caregivers who are making a difference.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As many as 25 million Americans live with rare, undiagnosed conditions. Tonight, Nicole Estaphan meets individuals living with rare diseases – and the clinicians and researchers who are determined to find answers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: There is a national mental health crisis among young people, with girls and young women suffering in particular. Tonight one college student shares with Anthony Everett her personal story of struggling with severe depression and anxiety, and time spent in a crowded emergency room. A social worker who struggled to find help for her own daughter’s mental health crisis describes the critical shortage of care providers. Experts from Boston Children’s Hospital discuss the crisis-level need for more treatment options. Finally, we talk to experts who are finding new ways to connect with young people who need help.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we take you along to see the lights, hear the music, and spread the joy of the season. Erika Tarantal meets a woman whose full-time job is spreading holiday cheer by wrapping big hotels and office buildings in festive decor.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we take you along to see the lights, hear the music, and spread the joy of the season.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Some are tiny – and some aren’t even visible to the naked eye. Tonight Erika Tarantal looks at how very small things can have a very big impact on our lives. Erika meets a researcher studying the brains of tiny ants, as well as two friends who co-founded a cosmetics company that uses microscopic algae as a main ingredient. Then, at the MWRA’s Deer Island treatment plant, Erika gets a lesson in how “bugs” (beneficial bacteria) are used to lay waste to…waste.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In honor of Chronicle’s 40th anniversary, tonight we’re bringing you memorable moments from the “Main Streets and Back Roads of New England” – our show’s longest-running feature – and we’ll also hitch a ride in the Old Chevy for memories from “On the Road.” Prep for our Friday prime-time special with this road trip down memory lane.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: When it comes to wealth, transportation, property, health care – there are gaps in society. Tonight we talk to people who are working to bridge those gaps: An Ipswich farm provides low-cost access to fresh produce; a local man built an app to help people with physical disabilities navigate public spaces; a Lawrence nonprofit offers grants to businesses whose owners face barriers to capital. A look at the gaps that separate society, and ideas from individuals who are trying to close them.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: In Orlando, nothing is as it seems. For three special effects artists, it's all on the line as they compete to create an Orlando-inspired theme park character. Little do they know, things are about to get hairy.

programme.title: In Transit by Esquire

programme.title: Plate It!

programme.desc: A gripping documentary uncovering real stories of children loaded on trains, sent west, and presented “for the picking.” From 1854 to 1929, over 6,000 children landed in Iowa communities. Discover their emotional journeys and impact on the Midwest.

programme.title: Good Deed DIY
programme.desc: Teachers deserve the best! Monica races against the clock to transform a beloved educator’s staff lounge. Can she pull off this scholastic surprise?

programme.title: Weathering the Storm
programme.desc: On December 10, 2021, a violent tornado outbreak hit western Kentucky. It was the deadliest weather event in the history of the Commonwealth. But as families faced a bleak holiday season, hope emerged in surprising ways.

programme.title: The Freedom Center
programme.desc: Across centuries of struggle, freedom has carried many meanings, some painful, some powerful. This documentary pulls back the curtain on forgotten stories and surprising truths, leaving one question: what does freedom really look like today, and what might it become tomorrow?

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Manny Hernandez of Barrels and Boards in Raynham pushes the pace against Chef Ryan Dziki of Wild Fox Pierogi. Will the chefs be able to identify the right ingredients and replicate each other’s sauces before the clock runs out?

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: In historic Ybor City, different cultures bring out the best in one another. Enjoy an authentic Cuban sandwich or dine at a restaurant now on its fifth generation of ownership. After chicken yoga (yes, that’s a thing!), treat yourself to a hand-rolled cigar.

programme.title: Ballin' in Baltimore
programme.desc: Angel joins baseball legend, Ryan Ripken, for a home run day, exploring the best of Baltimore. They bat 100 at Ripken Stadium, learn about sea life at the National Aquarium, crush indoor rock climbing and show down in an epic cook-off.

programme.title: Hit Me With Your Best Spot
programme.desc: Our local experts know the best thrills at Fun Spot. Orlando’s top comedian braves the gravity-defying SkyCoaster, a monster DJ and actor can’t stop screaming on White Lightning, and an influencer leaves all her followers behind on the go-kart track.

programme.title: On The Job
programme.desc: Brendan explores a beautiful orange grove while giving a tour on a school bus monster truck, pampers some pals at a European beer spa, and works the late-shift to make the best Philly cheesesteak outside the City of Brotherly Love.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Amy, a 26-year-old single mom, takes a shot at three blind dates. Will things putt around with Jesus, will she make a splash with Quashon, or will she find sanctuary with Jake?

programme.title: Plate It!
programme.desc: Former teacher Craig Falk is hitting the (cook)books against former lawyer Laurie Boucher. Which of these second-career chefs best showcases their culinary skills to craft the perfect packed lunch?

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Get in and get out of your comfort zone in the mountains of western Massachusetts. From boozy margaritas and snug motels to exhilarating ziplines and thrilling coasters. This is the Berkshires.

programme.title: Wait, What Happened?
programme.desc: Frank Nicotero takes to the streets to quiz people on the wildest, wackiest news stories. In "Imagination Station," contestants answer questions for cash about witty co-workers, life-saving screenings and teaching telemarketers a lesson.

programme.title: What's On The Menu?
programme.desc: Loosen that belt: Today we're filling up on comforting classics. From thick focaccia pizza to the juiciest smoked brisket, we guarantee there won't be room for dessert.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A teenager survives the unspeakable in her Elk Grove, California, home. When her younger sister goes missing, investigators question her about what she witnessed and uncover a tragedy that will shape her path from trauma to truth.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: These criminals may not have blood on their hands, but they still leave despair in their wake. From shady non-profits to fraudulent contractors, to wide-spread postal theft, here are some of the most devastating white-collar crimes to be attempted across the country.

programme.title: Free Spirit
programme.desc: Master bartender Kate Gerwin visits Kansas City to talk whiskey, taste the nonalcoholic future at an innovative new bar, experience jazz at the Mutual Musicians Foundation, and learn all about Prohibition in the city that ignored it best.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Stylishly single Jade is new to Sacramento dating scene and looking for love. Three exciting singles will try to impress her with throwing down at pottery, a day with animals and aerial silks.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: A duo pulls a Bonnie and Clyde in a truck stolen from a dealership, and a pair takes money from a monastery. These shocking stories of couples in crime have to be witnessed to be believed.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A man vanishes from a boarding house in 1988. The fight to find him uncovers one of the most shocking cases in Sacramento history and the surprising revelation of a grandmotherly-looking serial killer hiding in plain sight.
